name: Zinc
dosage: 15-45 mg elemental daily (acne: 25-30 mg as picolinate with 2-3 mg copper)
pregnancy_safe: Generally safe at RDA levels (11 mg elemental). Avoid high-dose supplementation without physician oversight.

name: Omega-3 EPA/DHA
dosage: 1,000-3,000 mg EPA+DHA combined daily (acne: 2,000 mg; anti-inflammatory threshold ~2,000 mg)
interactions: Warfarin, aspirin, clopidogrel (monitor INR/bleeding at >3 g/day)
Blood pressure medications (may potentiate hypotensive effects)
pregnancy_safe: Generally safe and beneficial. Limit to ≤3,000 mg EPA+DHA combined. Essential for fetal neural development (DHA).

name: Vitamin A
dosage: ≤5,000-10,000 IU retinol daily (short-term, monitored); prefer dietary sources
interactions: Isotretinoin/tretinoin (additive toxicity — NEVER combine without dermatologist)
pregnancy_safe: CONTRAINDICATED. Do NOT use retinol supplements if pregnant, planning pregnancy, or breastfeeding. Beta-carotene is safe but ineffective for acne.

name: Probiotics
dosage: 10-50 billion CFU daily (acne: 25-50B multi-strain; eczema: 25-50B with LGG)
pregnancy_safe: Generally considered safe; LGG specifically studied in pregnancy for eczema prevention in offspring.

name: DIM (Diindolylmethane)
dosage: 200-300 mg standard DIM or 150 mg BR-DIM daily
benefits: May reduce sebum production in hormonal acne
interactions: CYP450 inducers (may interact with various medications)
pregnancy_safe: NOT RECOMMENDED — hormonal effects unknown; avoid.

name: Saw Palmetto
dosage: 320 mg standardized extract daily (85-95% fatty acids and sterols)
pregnancy_safe: NOT RECOMMENDED — anti-androgen effects; avoid.

name: Spearmint
dosage: 2 cups strong tea daily (steep 5-10 min) OR 400-600 mg capsules
interactions: Liver-metabolized drugs (menthol compounds may affect CYP enzymes)
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; avoid high doses. Tea in moderation likely safe.

name: Berberine
dosage: 500 mg 2-3 times daily (1,000-1,500 mg total); start once daily and titrate up
interactions: CYP3A4 substrates (many medications — berberine inhibits this enzyme)
pregnancy_safe: CONTRAINDICATED — may cause uterine contractions; kernicterus risk in newborns.

name: Vitamin C
dosage: 500-1,000 mg daily (skin health); 1,000 mg daily for hyperpigmentation; photoprotection 500 mg 2x/day with vitamin E
interactions: Iron (significantly enhances absorption — separate if iron overload concern)
Warfarin (high-dose may reduce efficacy — monitor INR)
pregnancy_safe: Safe at standard doses (85-120 mg RDA). Avoid megadoses (>2,000 mg).

name: Niacinamide
dosage: 500 mg 1-2 times daily (up to 1,000 mg/day); topical niacinamide (2-10%) is first-line, oral is adjunct
pregnancy_safe: Safe at standard doses; essential nutrient.

name: Glutathione
dosage: 250-500 mg liposomal glutathione daily (oral only; do NOT recommend IV)
benefits: Master antioxidant (reduces oxidative stress driving pigmentation)
Promotes melanin shift from eumelanin to pheomelanin
interactions: Chemotherapy (may interfere with treatments — avoid during chemotherapy)
pregnancy_safe: Avoid — insufficient safety data.

name: Tranexamic Acid
dosage: 250-500 mg 2-3 times daily (500-1,500 mg total); melasma: 250 mg BID preferred for safety
interactions: Hormonal contraceptives (increased thrombosis risk — avoid combination)
Anticoagulants (may reduce efficacy or increase bleeding)
pregnancy_safe: CONTRAINDICATED — potential thrombotic risk.

name: Marine Collagen
dosage: 5-10 g daily of Type I/III hydrolyzed collagen; hair growth: 5-10 g daily
benefits: Provides building blocks for collagen synthesis
Stimulates fibroblasts to produce new collagen
Bioactive peptides may have signaling effects
Provides amino acid building blocks for keratin
pregnancy_safe: Generally considered safe; food-derived. Fish/shellfish allergy caution for marine sources.

name: CoQ10 (Coenzyme Q10)
dosage: 100-200 mg ubiquinol daily (or 200-400 mg ubiquinone if under 40)
interactions: Statins (CoQ10 depleted by statins — supplementation essential for statin users)
Chemotherapy (antioxidant effects may interfere — consult oncologist)
Blood pressure meds (may potentiate hypotensive effects)
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; generally considered safe but consult physician.

name: Astaxanthin
dosage: 4-6 mg daily (general); 6-12 mg daily (skin/UV protection)
interactions: Anticoagulants (very high doses may have mild antiplatelet effects — rare at standard doses)
Hormonal therapies (may affect hormone metabolism — limited evidence)
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; likely safe at 4-6 mg but consult physician.

name: Resveratrol
dosage: 250-500 mg trans-resveratrol daily (micronized or with piperine for absorption)
pregnancy_safe: NOT RECOMMENDED — limited safety data; theoretical hormonal effects.

name: Hyaluronic Acid
dosage: 120-200 mg daily
benefits: May reduce appearance of fine lines
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; generally considered safe but consult physician.

name: Evening Primrose Oil
dosage: 1,000-2,000 mg EPO daily (provides 80-200 mg GLA); children: 500-1,000 mg
benefits: Some eczema patients have impaired delta-6-desaturase
interactions: Anticoagulants (potential additive effect at high doses)
pregnancy_safe: Generally safe; GLA present in breast milk naturally. Avoid high doses.

name: Vitamin D3
dosage: 2,000-4,000 IU daily (adjust based on 25(OH)D levels); maintenance 1,000-2,000 IU
pregnancy_safe: Essential; safe at standard doses (4,000-6,000 IU for lactating mothers).

name: Quercetin
dosage: 500-1,000 mg standard daily; quercetin phytosome 250-500 mg; EMIQ 200-400 mg
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; avoid high doses.

name: Biotin
dosage: 2,500-10,000 mcg daily (only if deficiency suspected); RDA 30 mcg
interactions: LAB TESTS: causes false results on TSH, troponin, HCG, many immunoassays — stop 48-72 hours before blood work
pregnancy_safe: Safe; RDA increases to 35 mcg during lactation. CRITICAL: stop before blood work.

name: Iron
dosage: 25-65 mg elemental iron 1-2 times daily (only if deficient); ferritin target for hair growth >40 ng/mL (ideally 70-100)
benefits: Essential for DNA synthesis in rapidly dividing hair follicle cells
Supports oxygen delivery to hair follicles
Ferritin directly involved in hair follicle cycling
interactions: Thyroid medication (separate by 4 hours — iron reduces levothyroxine absorption)
Antibiotics (tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones, penicillamine — separate by 2-4 hours)
pregnancy_safe: Iron needs increase; standard prenatal contains 27-30 mg. Safe at standard doses. Do NOT take without confirmed deficiency.

name: Bovine Colostrum
dosage: 2-4g powder daily (standard); 1-2g of 40% IgG concentrate (optimized). Morning, empty stomach or with light meal.
benefits: Rich in immunoglobulins (IgG, IgA) — systemic immune modulation
Lactoferrin — antimicrobial (iron chelation), anti-inflammatory, barrier support
Growth factors (IGF-1, TGF-β) — tissue repair, wound healing, collagen synthesis
interactions: No known drug interactions at standard doses
pregnancy_safe: Limited data; theoretical benefit but insufficient safety studies. Consult physician before use during pregnancy or breastfeeding.
timing: Morning, empty stomach or with light meal
evidence_notes: Most evidence is for athletic performance, gut barrier repair, and immune support. Direct skin-specific RCTs are limited. Lactoferrin isolates have stronger acne evidence than whole colostrum. Evidence grade C = small trials, observational studies, expert opinion.
quality_markers: Third-party tested (heavy metals, pathogens, IgG verification)

private_label_suppliers: Sovereign Labs — bulk Colostrum-LD; MOQ ~100 units
APS BioGroup — Arizona-based; supplies many brands
Ingredient Depot — wholesale colostrum; flexible MOQ
